verdict_reasoning: TMED9 (p24alpha2) is a member of the p24/TMED family of type I transmembrane cargo receptors resident in the ER-Golgi compartments. Its TM domain faces the lumenal/secretory side, but the protein cycles within the COPI/COPII vesicle system and is resident in ER and Golgi membranes — not the plasma membrane. No CD designation. The single literature hit (PMID 39766158) associates TMED9 with extracellular vesicle formation but as a cargo-sorting regulator, not as a PM surface protein. Pre-no checklist: no documented cancer/cell-death ecto-pool; no germline/tissue-restricted PM display; not a lysosomal TM protein undergoing exocytosis; no documented minority PM pool or secretory cycling to the PM; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering at the outer leaflet. EV association reflects intracellular sorting function, not outer-leaflet display. The protein is an endomembrane-resident TM protein throughout its lifecycle.
